cards.title: Steal a Brainrot Tycoon: Vault Wars
cards.tagline: Build the vault. Steal their brainrots.
cards.lane: steal-brainrot / Tycoon (tycoon x brainrot untapped combo) — OPEN, top niche score 78.9, hot meta
cards.theme: Brainrot meme characters (the dominant 2026 Roblox meme aesthetic) live in a personal money-printing factory you defend and raid. Goofy, loud, instantly legible to a young audience.
cards.core_loop: Roll/hatch a random brainrot unit (common to secret, visible luck stat) -> place it on a conveyor pedestal where it auto-generates offline cash -> spend cash on more pedestals, luck, and DEFENSES (lasers, cages, guards) -> sneak into rivals' bases during a raid window and STEAL their highest-rarity brainrot -> bring it home to boost income -> rebirth for permanent luck/cash multipliers and new themed factory worlds. Steal-and-defend is the retention engine; roll-for-rarity is the dopamine.
cards.first_30_seconds: Spawn onto a tiny starter base with one cheap pedestal already printing cash and a giant glowing ROLL button. Tap it, get a goofy brainrot with a number floating over its head, watch cash tick up. A floating arrow points at a neighbor's base: 'STEAL THEIR BRAINROT ->'. You sprint over, grab one, a siren wails, you run home. Roll, place, steal, defend — delivered in 30s.
cards.depth: Three interacting systems give a 100+ hour ceiling: economy optimization (which pedestals/luck to invest in), base layout as a defense puzzle (maze guards, hide your best unit), and read-the-lobby raiding (who's AFK, when the window opens). Emergent metagame: steal-alliances and revenge feuds. The defend-vs-greed tension (more printers or more locks?) stays interesting as rarities and counter-defenses escalate.
cards.why_now: Steal-brainrot is the single hottest lane in the data: 403,082 de-contaminated Tycoon-lane demand, leader 40.6%, flagged OPEN and 'hot meta' (78.9, top niche score in the brief). tycoon x brainrot is explicitly an UNTAPPED combo (popular, ~never combined). roll 1.21, lucky 1.30, build 1.32, defense 1.53, and hide 1.58 all over-index in 1k+ winners — this loop stacks all five. The steal meta proves players love the raid tension; nobody has fused it with a real offline-tycoon spine.
cards.incumbent: 'Steal a Brainrot' (genre-defining 40.6% lane leader). Beat it by adding the persistent TYCOON base + RNG-hatch collection dex it lacks — turning a grab-and-run loop into a build-defend-collect game with offline progression that retains non-PvP players.
cards.differentiation: Current steal-brainrot games are shallow grab-loops with weak base persistence. This makes your base a real tycoon you grow and defend offline, units a rarity-dex (Slime-RNG style), and adds a defense-layout puzzle. It serves both the PvP raider AND the chill collector — doubling the retained audience.
cards.retention: Daily login luck streak, weekly new brainrot rarity drops + new factory world, limited 'heist events' (double-steal weekends), rebirth ladder, collection dex with set bonuses. The steal/defend feud loop is inherently social and self-renewing.
cards.monetization: Luck gamepass, auto-collect/auto-roll gamepass, VIP defense slot, limited event-exclusive brainrot gacha, 2x cash and 2x luck (group-friendly), rebirth-skip — all accelerators, none gate the core fun.
cards.virality: Steal-and-revenge is intrinsically social (raid friends, they raid back); group luck/cash multipliers reward squads; rare-steal moments are clip-and-thumbnail bait ('I STOLE his SECRET brainrot'). 'Steal a Brainrot' is a proven high-CTR thumbnail formula.

evidence_games.name: Steal-brainrot lane leader (de-contaminated)
evidence_games.note: Dominant Tycoon-lane demand for the steal meta; leader 40.6%, top niche score 78.9, flagged OPEN and hot. Verified verbatim in brief.

evidence_games.name: tycoon x brainrot combo (untapped)
evidence_games.note: Listed in UNTAPPED COMBOS: both ingredients popular, ~never combined, zero combined titles. Blue space on a proven-hot meta — no positive proof of fusion yet, only of each half.

evidence_games.name: Run a Restaurant! (offline-serve tycoon proof)
evidence_games.note: DERIVED: restaurant 93,182 x 22% and cooking 69,332 x 30% both imply ~19-21k CCU. Proves fresh offline-income tycoons clear 1k fast; the tycoon spine is well-trodden, only the brainrot+steal fusion is new.

cards.title: Mutation Diner: Roll the Ingredient
cards.lane: cooking (Incremental Simulator x Tycoon) — OPEN, 64.5 score, leader ~30%, 11 winners incl. 1 fresh
cards.theme: A cozy roadside diner where you don't roll dishes — you roll MUTATIONS on a single base ingredient. A plain tomato can become Golden (x10), Rainbow (x50), or a 1-in-1M Galaxy tomato that pays for your whole kitchen.
cards.core_loop: Tap the Mutation Burner to roll a mutation on your ingredient (visible luck stat, escalating rarity) -> place mutated ingredients into auto-cooking stations that earn offline -> spend on better burners (rarity odds), luck potions, and new kitchen-worlds -> rebirth for permanent luck/multipliers -> complete the recipe-dex for income buffs. Pure Slime-RNG roll->collect->upgrade->unlock.
cards.first_30_seconds: You're handed one boring tomato and a glowing burner. ROLL -> the tomato shimmers GOLD with a rarity beam and an x10 popup -> drop it in the pan, watch cash fountain up. 'Roll again?' Free second roll hits Rainbow -> screen flashes, number goes huge. Hooked on the slot-machine before any tutorial text.
cards.depth: The Build A Ring Farm pattern: the mutation multiplier creates an exponential payout curve where luck investment, rarity odds, and rebirth timing form the optimization spine. Recipe-dex set bonuses reward completionists; world unlocks gate new mutation pools. Friction-free 'roll = instant reward' keeps cooking as a flex/collection layer, not a gate — the roll is the dopamine, the kitchen is the flex.
cards.why_now: Incremental Simulator x Tycoon is the brief's #1 untapped combo (512 vs 451 games, ZERO combined). Cooking lane is OPEN (64.5, Tycoon-dominant, fragmented, leader ~30%, 11 winners incl. 1 fresh). 'Build A Ring Farm' — a grow-sell incremental with the exact mutation-multiplier layer — hit ~40k CCU at 61 days, proving the mutation hook scales fast past 1k. No cooking game runs that engine. Purest fit for the user's Slime-RNG + cooking taste.
cards.incumbent: Catch and Cook! (~1.3k @102d) is the closest verified food-roll comp — and a warning: food-specific roll games cluster near a soft ~1.3k ceiling. The thesis depends on porting the ~40k Build A Ring Farm mutation engine onto food, not on existing food-roll comps.
cards.differentiation: Existing cooking incrementals roll DISHES (flat rarity). This rolls MUTATIONS with stacking multipliers — the exponential-payout pattern that let Build A Ring Farm hit ~40k where dish-rollers cluster at ~1.3k. The single-ingredient focus is instantly legible to a young audience: everyone understands 'make the tomato golden.'
cards.retention: Daily luck-boost login, weekly new mutation tiers + limited mythic mutations, recipe-dex completion, rebirth prestige ladder, kitchen-world unlocks pacing progression past 100h.
cards.monetization: 2x luck, 2x cash, auto-roll, and offline-time gamepasses; limited premium mutation banners (event-only Galaxy odds); daily streak rewards. Core roll always free — luck only accelerates the chase.
cards.virality: Group luck multiplier when playing with friends; insane-mutation clips are the thumbnail (a giant glowing Galaxy tomato, 'x1,000,000?!'). Display shelf for show-off/trade.

evidence_games.name: Build A Ring Farm
evidence_games.note: Grow-sell incremental with a mutation-multiplier layer, ~40k @61d (brief, vetted Concept #1) — direct proof the mutation engine scales fast past 1k. NOTE: adjacent (farming, not cooking).

evidence_games.name: Catch and Cook!
evidence_games.note: Closest verified food-roll comp, ~1.3k @102d. Real but a soft ~1.3k ceiling — the key risk for the food-specific framing this concept must break.

evidence_games.name: Sell Lemons (via vetted Concept #1)
evidence_games.note: ~99k food-stand sell loop cited in the brief — shows food-stand Tycoon demand exists; supporting, not a direct mutation-engine comp.

cards.title: Steal a Chef: Restaurant Heist Tycoon
cards.tagline: Cook, get rich, steal their chefs
cards.lane: restaurant / cooking (Tycoon) — OPEN, 78.1 score, 93,182 demand, leader ~22%, ~19-21 winners
cards.theme: Run a neon fast-food empire where your roster of rolled chefs is the prize — and rival players can break in and STEAL your best chef off the line if you leave it unguarded.
cards.core_loop: Roll random chefs (common -> mythic, visible luck stat) -> assign them to cooking stations where they auto-serve dishes for offline cash -> spend cash on roll tickets, luck potions, station upgrades, and security locks -> raid other players' restaurants to steal undefended chefs, or defend your own -> rebirth for permanent luck/cash multipliers and chef-dex set bonuses.
cards.first_30_seconds: Spawn in front of a single empty grill. Big glowing 'ROLL CHEF' button -> first free roll spits a chef out with a rarity beam and screen shake -> plop them on the grill, watch the first $ tick up in 3 seconds. A floating arrow points at a rival's kitchen: 'A Legendary chef is cooking unguarded — go steal it.'
cards.depth: Three interacting economies: the offline-tycoon cash curve, the gacha rarity ladder (luck investment vs payout), and the risk/reward steal-vs-defend layer that makes idle income contestable. Defense placement, chef positioning, and rebirth timing create genuine optimization; the steal layer adds a social PvP skill ceiling. Stays interesting because the meta is other players, not a fixed curve.
cards.why_now: Restaurant Tycoon is independently OPEN (score 78.1, demand 93,182, ~19-21 winners, leader 'Run a Restaurant!' only ~22% share). Steal-brainrot is the hottest meta (403,082, OPEN). UNTAPPED COMBOS explicitly names 'tycoon x brainrot' as popular-but-never-combined. No restaurant tycoon in the data carries a steal layer — this fuses the hottest open mechanic onto a proven-fragmented food lane, with chef gacha serving the user's cooking + RNG taste.
cards.incumbent: Run a Restaurant! — restaurant-lane leader at only ~22% (30% in cooking lane), a pure build/serve tycoon with NO gacha and NO steal layer. Its fragmented share is exactly the room for a differentiated entrant.
cards.differentiation: Every 1k+ game in the restaurant lane is a flat build-and-serve tycoon. This is the only one where your income source (chefs) is both a collectible AND contestable by other players — importing the steal-brainrot dopamine (~400k demand, adjacent lane) onto a fragmented food lane that lacks it entirely.
cards.retention: Daily login chef-roll, chef-dex completion set bonuses, weekly new chef banners (limited rares), leaderboard for richest restaurant / most chefs stolen, rebirth prestige. The steal layer gives a reason to log in and re-secure your roster.
cards.monetization: Auto-collect + auto-defend gamepass, 2x luck and 2x cash gamepasses, VIP instant-lock security, limited premium chef gacha banners, daily reward streak. Nothing gates the core roll — luck/defense only accelerate.
cards.virality: Group luck/cash multiplier when friends play; raiding is inherently social ('come help me steal his Mythic chef'); steal clips are high-CTR. Thumbnail: a chef yanked out of a kitchen with a giant red STOLEN stamp.

evidence_games.name: Run a Restaurant!
evidence_games.note: Restaurant/cooking lane leader at only ~22%/30% share — proves the food-tycoon fantasy sustains 1k+ winners while staying fragmented and OPEN, with no gacha/steal layer to defend against. Derived from 93,182 x 22%.

evidence_games.name: Steal-brainrot lane (de-contaminated demand)
evidence_games.note: Hottest open mechanic in the data, leader 40.6%; 'tycoon x brainrot' explicitly listed as never-combined. Lane demand, not a single game's CCU.

evidence_games.name: Restaurant lane (de-contaminated demand)
evidence_games.note: Score 78.1, ~19-21 winners at 1k+, OPEN — proves entrants repeatedly clear 1k in this fragmented food lane. Lane demand, not a single game's CCU.

cards.title: Unbox a Tycoon: Crate Factory
cards.tagline: Open crates, build a loot empire, rebirth
cards.lane: unboxing (Tycoon) — OPEN, most fragmented leader of any lane (23%), 21,261 de-contaminated demand
cards.theme: You run a crate-opening factory. Conveyor belts feed mystery crates into your unboxer; what pops out (pets, gems, relics across a rarity ladder) lands on display pedestals that generate passive income. Richer collection wall -> faster belts -> bigger crates. Industrial-arcade loot factory you physically grow on a plot.
cards.core_loop: Open crate (RNG reveal) -> collected item auto-displays and generates idle cash by rarity -> cash upgrades belt speed, crate tier, and luck -> unlock new factory wings (Pets -> Gems -> Mythic Beasts -> Cosmic) -> rebirth to a prestige factory for permanent luck+income multiplier. The Slime-RNG collection loop with a buildable Tycoon plot as the display surface.
cards.first_30_seconds: You walk onto an empty factory plot with one crate and a giant ROLL button. First crate is rigged to an 'Epic' pet that flies to a pedestal and immediately starts spitting visible coins. A second locked crate tier glints right next to you. Collect -> idle income -> visible next unlock, all in the first 20 seconds.
cards.depth: Two intersecting economies: the RNG collection (chase rarity, complete sets) and the Tycoon layout (placement matters — adjacency bonuses, themed-wing multipliers, belt-routing optimization). Players optimize a physical factory build AND a collection. Prestige resets add a meta-layer of luck-stat investment.
cards.why_now: unboxing is OPEN with the most fragmented leader of any lane (23% — vs garden 59%, pet 58%, merge 76%) and a real 21,261 demand after stripping 93% mega-title bleed (honest figure). It sits in Tycoon and directly executes the flagged Incremental-Simulator x Tycoon untapped combo (512 vs 451, 0 combined) — the biggest structural arbitrage in the brief. roll 1.21, lucky 1.30, build 1.32, tower 1.49 all over-index in hits.
cards.incumbent: No named in-lane benchmark exists — the unboxing leader is unnamed at 23%, which is both the opportunity and the risk (no proven 1k+ title to study in-lane). The studyable cross-lane benchmark is 'Run a Restaurant!'-style Tycoon retention applied to an unboxing reveal.
cards.differentiation: Most unboxing games are pure case-openers (open, see number, repeat) with thin retention; most tycoons lack the RNG dopamine. This makes the loot you unbox INTO the income engine of a buildable factory — reveal and tycoon are the same object. No combined Incremental-Sim x Tycoon game exists in the corpus, so this is uncontested whitespace.
cards.retention: Daily free crate (streak-scaled luck), weekly limited crate theme, prestige/rebirth ladder, set-completion display bonuses, leaderboard for factory income/hour, friends-server group-luck multiplier. New factory wing every weekly update feeds the unlock treadmill.
cards.monetization: 2x Luck pass, Auto-Open pass (idle crate opening), 2x Income pass, limited weekly premium crates (gacha), rebirth-skip token, VIP pedestal slots, cosmetic factory skins. No fun gated — luck passes change odds-to-good, not access.
cards.virality: 'Secret pet' reveals are inherently shareable; a wall of rare loot is a screenshot flex. Group-luck draws friends. Thumbnail of a rainbow crate exploding into a giant mythic with 'I GOT THE 1-IN-A-MILLION' is proven high-CTR. Trading (later) adds social stickiness.

evidence_games.name: Unboxing-lane leader (23%, unnamed)
evidence_games.note: DERIVED, not measured: ~23% of the 21,261 de-contaminated demand. Most fragmented leader of any OPEN lane = low bar to top, but no named comp to study in-lane.

evidence_games.name: Merge a Nuke!
evidence_games.note: DERIVED (~76% of 35,249 merge demand). Caveat: this is the MONOLITH the brief says AVOID; cited only as weak proof a single-mechanic Tycoon reveal can hold 5-figure CCU, not as a model to copy.

evidence_games.name: Incremental-Sim x Tycoon combo (untapped)
evidence_games.note: Brief's flagged #1 arbitrage: 512 vs 451 games, 0 combined. Structural whitespace this card executes — novelty is the upside and the unproven-retention risk.

cards.title: Grow a Spice Garden: Mutation Kitchen
cards.tagline: Grow, roll mutations, cook, sell, defend
cards.lane: restaurant/food-stand (Tycoon) — OPEN, 93,182 demand, leader 22%; rides garden 821k + steal 403k
cards.theme: A cozy food-garden where you grow ingredients, but each harvest ROLLS a mutation (Golden, Rainbow, On-Fire, Frozen, Giant) that sets its value. You don't just sell raw crops — you COOK mutated ingredients into signature dishes at your food stand (the wedge that makes this a food-stand identity, not a generic garden). A light steal/defend layer lets players raid neighbors' ripe golden crops and fence their own.
cards.core_loop: Plant seed -> auto-grow -> HARVEST triggers a mutation roll (RNG) -> COOK the mutated ingredient into a dish at your stand -> sell for cash scaled by mutation rarity x recipe tier -> buy luck, plots, rarer seeds, recipes, fences -> unlock new ingredient/dish tiers -> rebirth for permanent mutation-luck multiplier. Slime-RNG collection (the mutation index) bolted onto a cooking-stand tycoon with a steal-brainrot social spike.
cards.first_30_seconds: You get one plot and a seed. It auto-grows in ~8s; first harvest is rigged to a 'Golden' mutation (gold burst, '+x5', screen pop), auto-cooks into your first dish, auto-sells, cash rockets, and a second plot unlocks for a few coins. The mutation index flashes '1/120 discovered'. Collect, cook, upgrade — under 30 seconds.
cards.depth: Seed strain x growth condition x mutation x recipe forms a deep optimization board; some strains bias toward certain mutations and some dishes multiply specific rarities, so players engineer combos. Sell-timing vs steal-risk adds social tension. Mutation index (120+ entries) plus recipe set bonuses and rebirth luck-stat investment sustain long-tail play. NOTE: scope is heavy — four fused systems (garden sim, RNG index, cooking, steal/defend); each must be cut to its minimum viable form to ship.
cards.why_now: Rides high-demand signals — garden (821k) and steal-brainrot (403k, hot meta) — while landing in the OPEN restaurant/food-stand Tycoon lane (93,182, leader only 22%). The proven-but-noisy lead harvest×steal / garden×sell -> 'Grow a Garden 2' (508k) confirms the mutation-harvest loop works at massive scale, BUT that scale belongs to the garden monolith; we capture it by re-skinning to cooking and wedging the fragmented food-stand lane. lucky 1.30, survive 1.21, defense 1.53, roll 1.21, build 1.32 all over-index.
cards.incumbent: Grow a Garden 2 (59% of garden lane, 508k) is the monolith — do NOT fight head-on. Beat the fragmented food-stand leader 'Run a Restaurant!' (22% of 93k) by owning the 'RNG mutation + cooking-sell' niche rather than competing as a general garden.
cards.differentiation: Grow a Garden 2 is a general-purpose plant-collect garden; this narrows hard into a COOKING/food-stand identity — you grow to cook and sell DISHES, not collect raw plants — and fuses the steal social spike. Mutation-roll-as-core (vs garden's plant-collect) is the Slime-RNG hook the monolith underweights. The cooking layer is load-bearing: without it this is just a Grow-a-Garden clone fighting a 59% monolith, so the dish/recipe system must be real, not cosmetic.
cards.retention: Daily seed (streak luck), weekly limited seed strain + mutation, steal/defend social loop (revenge mechanic), rebirth ladder, mutation-index and recipe set bonuses, friends-server group-luck multiplier, seasonal mutation events. The steal mechanic creates organic 'come back to check my crops' habit.
cards.monetization: 2x Mutation-Luck pass, Auto-Harvest/Auto-Cook pass, 2x Sell pass, theft-shield gamepass, limited premium seed crates (gacha weekly), rebirth-skip, VIP plot slots. Fun ungated; passes accelerate luck and idle, not access to mutations.
cards.virality: Stealing a friend's Rainbow crop is a built-in clip moment; 'steal' is the hottest meta (403k). Mutation reveals are screenshot bait. Thumbnail of a giant rainbow carrot with 'I STOLE THE 1-IN-A-MILLION CROP' targets both garden and steal audiences. Group-luck and revenge loops drive friend-pull.

evidence_games.name: Grow a Garden 2
evidence_games.note: Validates mutation-harvest loop at huge scale (garden leader, 59%) — but a monolith to wedge around via cooking re-skin, NOT to fight.

evidence_games.name: Steal-brainrot leader
evidence_games.note: Derived: 40.6% of 403,082 steal-brainrot demand. Hot steal meta — the social spike layer borrows proven demand.

evidence_games.name: Run a Restaurant!
evidence_games.note: Food-stand Tycoon lane leader at only 22% of 93,182 — the actual OPEN target to top. Reached only if the cooking layer is real, not just framing.

cards.title: Hatch a Pet: Roll, Steal & Trade Tycoon
cards.tagline: Hatch rare pets. Steal your friend's.
cards.lane: pet (Pet Care, 431k demand) — CROSS-LANE bet: NOT open, Adopt Me holds 58% (concentrated). Play is importing the steal-meta into pet-hatch RNG.
cards.theme: A cozy pet ranch where you hatch adorable rare pets that earn coins, with a daring steal-and-trade layer that turns rare pets into social currency.
cards.core_loop: Tap an egg to hatch a random pet (common to secret, visible luck/rarity) -> pets passively earn coins on your ranch (offline) -> spend coins on better eggs (rarity odds), luck potions, ranch expansions -> open a timed STEAL window to snatch a rival's displayed pet (or safely TRADE for one) -> chase dex completion for permanent income set-bonuses -> rebirth for luck/coin multipliers and new biome-themed egg worlds. Pure Slime-RNG roll->collect->upgrade->unlock with steal/trade as the social hook.
cards.first_30_seconds: You spawn on a small ranch with one egg glowing on a pedestal and a coin counter. Tap: the egg wobbles, cracks, a cute pet pops out with a rarity burst and a coin trickle starts. A banner: 'A rare pet is on display next door — STEAL or TRADE?' One tap to roll again, one tap to peek next door. Roll-collect-earn lands in under 20 seconds, instantly legible to a young audience.
cards.depth: Depth lives in the economy and social layer: optimizing luck-vs-income reinvestment, dex set-bonus planning (keep vs fodder), and a player-driven trade market where rare pets accrue social value. The steal window adds risk management (display your best pet for flex, or vault it for safety?). Scales via deepening rarity tiers, mutations, and rebirth multipliers — a proven incremental ceiling — while staying legible.
cards.why_now: Honest correction: pet is NOT flagged open — it's a single Pet Care lane (431,563) with Adopt Me at 58% (concentrated, monolith-leaning). The thesis is to import the hot steal-meta (403k) and the proven harvest×steal pattern (Grow a Garden 2, 821k) into a pet-hatch RNG loop — the PUREST fit for the user's Slime-RNG taste reskinned to pets. roll 1.21, lucky 1.30, hide 1.58 support the RNG + steal/stealth layer. Risk: entering against a 58% incumbent, so differentiation must carry it.
cards.incumbent: 'Adopt Me!' (58% of Pet lane) — a pet-CARE/roleplay/trading game, NOT a roll-incremental. 58% means concentrated, not open; beating it head-on is hard. Realistic targets are smaller pet-hatch incrementals (solo coin-grind, no social steal stakes) — beat THEM by fusing the cleanest Slime-RNG loop with a steal/trade social layer and a generous dex-driven gacha.
cards.differentiation: Adopt Me is trading + roleplay with no incremental dopamine; pet-hatch sims are solo coin-grinds with no social stakes. This sits in the gap: a tight roll-collect-upgrade incremental (the loop the user loves) with an optional steal/trade layer that makes rare pets socially meaningful, and a dex with income set-bonuses rewarding completion over pure luck. Differentiation must be sharp because the leader is at 58%.
cards.retention: Daily luck streak, weekly new pet/egg rarity drops and biome worlds, limited event eggs, a live trade economy that self-sustains engagement, rebirth ladder, dex set-bonus chase. Trading + stealing keep friend groups checking each other's ranches.
cards.monetization: Luck gamepass, auto-hatch, extra pet slots, VIP egg, limited event-pet gacha, 2x coins / 2x luck (group-buyable), trade-slot expansion, pet-vault (anti-steal) pass. All accelerators — the dex and trade economy stay skill/social-driven.
cards.virality: Trading and stealing are inherently social and clip-worthy ('traded my secret pet', 'he STOLE my mythic'); group luck multipliers reward squads; rare-hatch reactions are thumbnail gold. The pet aesthetic is broadly appealing to the young Roblox audience.

evidence_games.name: Pet Care lane demand (concentrated, NOT flagged open)
evidence_games.note: Real number from brief, corrected: Pet Care lane with Adopt Me at 58% leader (concentrated). No 'Pet>Incremental sub-lane OPEN' flag exists. Large demand is real; openness is not.

evidence_games.name: Steal-brainrot meta (social steal hook)
evidence_games.note: Real (brief: 'hot meta, OPEN'). Proves the steal/snatch window is a hot, retained social mechanic — the genuine importable differentiator for this cross-lane bet.

evidence_games.name: Grow a Garden 2 (harvest x steal proven)
evidence_games.note: Real (brief: garden 821,241; harvest x steal a proven combo). Proves steal/trade-on-collectibles is a mega-scale retained pattern, de-risking the steal+trade layer.

cards.title: Hatch & Hold: Monster Tower Survival
cards.tagline: Hatch the army. Hold the night.
cards.lane: survive x hatch (untapped combo) / tower-defense survival
cards.theme: You defend a glowing crystal at the center of an island; waves of monsters attack each night, and you hatch your OWN monsters from eggs to fight for you.
cards.core_loop: Open eggs to hatch random monster defenders (common to mythic, visible power + luck stat) -> place them around your crystal as living towers -> survive escalating night waves that drop currency and rarer eggs -> spend on better eggs (rarity odds), hatch-luck, and unit upgrades/merges -> push wave milestones that unlock new biomes and egg tiers -> rebirth for permanent luck/damage multipliers and a collection dex. Roll -> place -> survive -> upgrade -> roll better.
cards.first_30_seconds: You land next to a glowing crystal with a single egg pulsing in front of you and a countdown: 'NIGHT 1 IN 0:20.' You tap the egg, it cracks, a cute monster pops out and auto-walks to a tower slot. The countdown hits zero, a small wave shuffles in, your monster zaps them, coins fountain out, and a 'NEW EGG UNLOCKED' banner pops. Roll-place-defend delivered before the first minute ends.
cards.depth: Tower-defense placement + unit synergy gives a real strategy layer (elemental counters, AoE vs single-target, buff/aura monsters, choke-point mazing) on top of the RNG collection chase. Co-op adds role specialization (tank/buff/nuke). The push-vs-bank tension (spend coins on this run's defense or save for a rarer egg?) and the merge/fodder economy give a deep, self-teaching loop that scales to 100+ hours via endless wave/biome escalation.
cards.why_now: survive x hatch is an explicit UNTAPPED combo — both parent tags popular yet ~never shipped together, so it's genuine white space. Both parents over-index among 1k+ winners: survive 1.21, stacked with tower 1.49, defense 1.53, monster 1.36, roll 1.21, lucky 1.30 — the most hit-correlated mechanic cluster in the dataset, all feeding one loop. Rides the proven hatch-RNG dopamine the user loves. Caveat: the fusion itself has zero shipped precedent — novelty is upside and unproven risk.
cards.incumbent: Closest analogs are pure tower-defense games (the Skibi/Toilet Tower Defense family — general knowledge, not in the brief) and pure pet-hatch sims. No single game owns 'hatch your own monsters AND survive tower-defense waves.' Beat TD incumbents by adding the addictive hatch-collection spine; beat hatch-sims by adding real survival stakes and co-op.
cards.differentiation: Tower-defense games hand you fixed shop units; this makes your roster an RNG-hatched, mergeable, rarity-chase collection — the Slime-RNG loop powering a TD game. Hatch-sims have no stakes; here your monsters can FAIL the wave, making each roll consequential. Co-op survival makes it social where most hatch-sims are solo grinds.
cards.retention: Endless escalating waves + biome unlocks give an infinite progression ladder, weekly new egg/monster drops and limited mythic events, daily luck streak, prestige/rebirth with permanent multipliers, a monster dex with set auras. Co-op nights pull friends back nightly.
cards.monetization: Luck/hatch-speed gamepass, auto-hatch, extra unit slots, VIP egg, limited event eggs (gacha), 2x coins and 2x luck (group-buyable), revive-the-crystal token. Accelerators only — placement and merging skill still matter, so spend never trivializes the fun.
cards.virality: Co-op 'we survived to night 100' clips, rare-hatch reactions, and squad luck multipliers drive play-with-friends spread. Monster designs are inherently meme/thumbnail-able. The wave-milestone leaderboard fuels competitive sharing.

evidence_games.name: survive x hatch combo (untapped, per brief)
evidence_games.note: Listed in brief UNTAPPED COMBOS: both parent tags popular, ~never combined, zero shipped fusion. Novelty is upside AND unproven retention risk. No 1k+ proof exists for the fusion.

evidence_games.name: Tower/defense tag lift among 1k+ winners
evidence_games.note: Brief gives LIFT not CCU: tower 1.49, defense 1.53 over-index in 1k+ winners — TD is a proven high-retention skeleton that de-risks the survival half.

evidence_games.name: Roll/lucky tag lift among 1k+ winners
evidence_games.note: Brief gives LIFT not CCU: roll 1.21, lucky 1.30 over-index in winners — proves hatch-RNG collection dopamine scales. Only the fusion of the two halves is unproven.

cards.title: Hide the Chef: Kitchen Hide & Seek Tycoon
cards.tagline: Hide your chefs. Cook before they're caught
cards.lane: cooking / restaurant (Tycoon x Hide) — OPEN, fragmented (leaders 22-30%, ~11-21 winners)
cards.theme: A chaotic restaurant where a roaming Health Inspector (NPC, scaling into player-controlled) tries to catch your illegal star chefs. Hide your rolled chefs in vents, walk-in freezers, and dumpster props — the longer they cook undiscovered, the bigger the payout.
cards.core_loop: Roll chefs from the gacha -> physically HIDE them around your kitchen in prop spots (high-traffic = more cash but easier to find) -> a Health Inspector patrols hunting them -> survive the round to bank multiplied cash, get caught and lose the chef's earnings -> reinvest in better hiding spots, decoys, luck, and new chefs -> rebirth for permanent multipliers.
cards.first_30_seconds: You roll a chef, then a giant arrow: 'HIDE HIM before the Inspector comes!' You drag the chef into a freezer — a countdown and a creeping flashlight appear. The Inspector rounds the corner, you hold your breath, he passes, CASH x3 pops. Legible tension in under 20 seconds.
cards.depth: Grafts the two highest-lift hit ingredients (hide 1.58, defense 1.53) onto a tycoon economy. Hiding-spot selection is a real risk/reward spatial puzzle layered over the gacha rarity chase and offline-cash curve. The seeker role (NPC scaling into player-controlled inspector) adds an asymmetric PvP skill ceiling. CAVEAT: no comp in the corpus carries both hide and cooking/tycoon tags, so transfer of those lifts to a food-tycoon economy is unproven — this is the core bet, not a settled mechanic.
cards.why_now: 'hide' (1.58) and 'defense' (1.53) are the #2 and #3 winning-ingredient tag lifts in the entire corpus (only car 1.68 is higher), yet no cooking/restaurant game uses them. Cooking and restaurant lanes are both OPEN and fragmented (leaders 22-30%, ~11-21 winners). Grafting the two most over-indexing hit mechanics onto a proven-open food lane that lacks any hide/seek tension is a clean white-space bet.
cards.incumbent: Run a Restaurant! (~20.5k CCU) — pure passive build/serve with zero tension or seeker loop; and broadly the hide-genre leaders that have no food/tycoon economy attached.
cards.differentiation: No restaurant tycoon has a hide-and-seek round loop; no hide-and-seek game has a cooking-tycoon economy. This is the only title where idle food income is gated behind active hide/defend tension — giving the lane the moment-to-moment thrill its passive incumbents lack, using the two highest-lift mechanics in the data. Risk: it is the only white-space here precisely because no proven comp validates the combo.
cards.retention: Round-based replay (each round a fresh hide puzzle), daily chef-roll, new hiding-spot prop packs weekly, Inspector difficulty ladder, rebirth prestige, leaderboard for longest-hidden chef / most rounds survived.
cards.monetization: 2x cash + 2x luck gamepasses, premium hiding-spot prop packs (cosmetic + slight edge), auto-hide VIP, decoy-pack gacha, daily streak. Core hide loop is free — paid spots accelerate but placement skill still decides outcomes.
cards.virality: Friends defend one kitchen co-op vs the Inspector (group multiplier); the hide/catch moment is the ideal clip and thumbnail (a chef yanked out of a vent, 'CAUGHT!'). Asymmetric seeker role drives 'play with me' invites.

evidence_games.name: Run a Restaurant!
evidence_games.note: Restaurant/cooking lane incumbent (leader 22-30% share) with zero tension loop — the passive lane this injects hide/seek thrill into. Verified in brief.

evidence_games.name: Catch and Cook!
evidence_games.note: Cooking incremental @102d at the ~1.3k soft ceiling — evidence a plain food hook caps near 1k and a stronger active mechanic is needed to break past it. Verified in brief.

evidence_games.name: hide / defense tag lift among 1k+ winners
evidence_games.note: Brief gives LIFT not CCU: hide 1.58 and defense 1.53 are the #2/#3 highest lifts in the corpus, absent from every food lane. The whole bet is whether those lifts transfer to a food-tycoon economy — unproven by any comp.
